","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/README.md","text":"# filesystem watcher

> Watches the tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes and syncs them into the wiki

Watches the tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes (created, modified, deleted by tools like Claude Code) and syncs them into the running wiki in real time.

## Key features

* **Live file sync** -- detects file changes via chokidar and updates the wiki store automatically
* **Reload alerts** -- yellow alert + red refresh icon for changes requiring a browser reload (module tiddlers, system tiddlers)
* **Loop prevention** -- hooks TW's save/delete functions to ignore its own filesystem writes
* **CLI mode skip** -- exits immediately when `TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE=1` so CLI imports don't hang
* **Ignored tiddlers** -- skips noisy tiddlers like `$:/StoryList` that change frequently and have no value for external sync
* **Configurable poll interval** -- set sync frequency in settings

## Prerequisites

* chokidar (npm dependency, installed with the wiki)

## Quick start

Install plugin. Configure the poll interval in ControlPanel > Settings > filesystem watcher. External file changes in the tiddlers directory sync automatically into the running wiki.

## Plugin Library

Install from the [rimir plugin library](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-plugin-library/) via *Control Panel → Plugins → Get more plugins*.

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md)

"},"$:/config/SyncPollingInterval":{"title":"$:/config/SyncPollingInterval","created":"20260309090645748","modified":"20260309090645748","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"2000
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/architecture","created":"20260305110636213","modified":"20260305110636213","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Data flow

Filesystem change → chokidar → `$tw.loadTiddlersFromFile()` → `wiki.addTiddler()` (or `wiki.deleteTiddler()` for removals)

!! Components

|!Component |!Layer |!Role |
|`watcher.js` |Server (Node.js) |Main module — chokidar watcher, file loading, loop prevention |
|`reload-indicator.js` |Browser |Sets `$:/temp/` flag + `body.fsw-reload-needed` class on alert |
|`styles.tid` |Browser CSS |Red refresh icon styling via `body.fsw-reload-needed` |
|`config.tid` |Config |Sets `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` |

!! Loop prevention

Hooks `saveTiddlerToFile` and `deleteTiddlerFile` with a write-lock. When TW itself writes a file, the lock prevents the watcher from re-importing it.

!! Syncer info update

After importing a tiddler, updates `$tw.syncer.tiddlerInfo[title].changeCount` to match the wiki's change count. This prevents the syncer from re-saving the tiddler back to disk.

!! Reload detection

System tiddlers (`$:/` prefix) and module tiddlers (`type: application/javascript` with `module-type` field) don't sync to the browser via the normal polling mechanism. The watcher creates an alert tiddler (tagged `$:/tags/Alert`) and the browser-side `reload-indicator.js` sets a `$:/temp/` flag + adds `fsw-reload-needed` CSS class to `document.body`.

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">The red refresh icon persists after dismissing the alert. Only a full page reload (F5) clears it.</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/dev-workflow":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/dev-workflow","created":"20260305110636213","modified":"20260305110636213","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"Dev Workflow","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Plugin files

|!File |!Role |
|`watcher.js` |Server-side startup module (chokidar watcher) |
|`reload-indicator.js` |Browser-side module (sets temp flag + body class) |
|`styles.tid` |CSS for red refresh icon |
|`config.tid` |Sets `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` |

!! Tiddler behavior

|!Tiddler type |!Sync behavior |!Alert? |
|Regular tiddler |Syncs live to browser (~4s) |No |
|System tiddler (`$:/`) |Loads on server, doesn't sync |Yes — yellow alert + red icon |
|Module tiddler (JS) |Loads on server, doesn't sync |Yes — yellow alert + red icon |

!! Development workflow

# Edit tiddler files in `mywiki/tiddlers/`
# Regular tiddlers appear in browser within the poll interval
# System/module tiddlers trigger alert + red refresh icon
# Dismiss alert (icon stays red)
# Click refresh button or press F5 to reload

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="tip">
<$fill $name="body">TiddlyWiki encodes `$:/` as `$__` in filenames on disk.</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Restart rules

|!Change |!Restart needed? |
|Edit `watcher.js` |Yes — JS module |
|Edit `reload-indicator.js` |Yes — JS module |
|Edit `styles.tid` or `config.tid` |No — filesystem-watcher syncs these |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/examples","created":"20260305110636214","modified":"20260305110636214","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Edit a tiddler in an external editor

# Open `mywiki/tiddlers/MyTiddler.tid` in VS Code (or any text editor)
# Change the `text:` content and save the file
# Within ~4 seconds (poll interval), the tiddler updates in the browser
# No server restart needed — the watcher detects the change via chokidar

!! Create a new tiddler from the filesystem

# Create a new file `mywiki/tiddlers/NewTiddler.tid`:

```
title: NewTiddler
tags: example

This is created from the filesystem.
```

# Save the file — the watcher picks it up and calls `$tw.wiki.addTiddler()`
# The tiddler appears in the browser after the next syncer poll

!! Delete a tiddler from the filesystem

# Delete `mywiki/tiddlers/MyTiddler.tid` from disk
# The watcher calls `$tw.wiki.deleteTiddler()` and cleans up `$tw.boot.files`
# The tiddler disappears from the browser after the next syncer poll

!! Bulk file operations

Edit or create multiple `.tid` files at once (e.g., via a script). Each file triggers a debounced (200ms) change event. All tiddlers sync to the browser within one poll interval.

!! System tiddler change (triggers reload alert)

# Edit a file that maps to a `$:/` title (filename starts with `$__`)
# The watcher loads it on the server and creates a yellow alert
# The browser shows the alert + red refresh icon
# Dismiss the alert — the red icon stays until you press F5

!! Adjusting the poll interval

Edit `config.tid` in the plugin directory to change `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval`:

```
title: $:/config/SyncPollingInterval
type: text/plain

2000
```

Lower values (e.g., 2000ms) mean faster sync but more HTTP requests.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/faq":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/faq","created":"20260305110636214","modified":"20260305110636214","rdt.order":"5","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"FAQ","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Q: Why don't system tiddlers sync to the browser?

TiddlyWiki's browser-side syncer polls `GET /recipes/default/tiddlers.json` which excludes `$:/` tiddlers by default. The config tiddler `$:/config/SyncSystemTiddlersFromServer` is `"no"` out of the box. The watcher loads them into `$tw.wiki` on the server, but the browser never fetches them. A full page reload (F5) is the only way to pick them up.

!! Q: How do I change the poll interval?

Edit `config.tid` in the plugin directory. It sets `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` (value in milliseconds). Default is `4000` (4 seconds). Lower values mean faster sync but more HTTP traffic.

!! Q: What if chokidar misses a change?

Chokidar uses `awaitWriteFinish` with a 200ms stability threshold. If a file is still being written when the threshold fires, it may read a partial file. In practice this is rare. If you suspect a missed change, save the file again or restart the server.

!! Q: Can I watch additional directories?

No. The watcher monitors only `$tw.boot.wikiTiddlersPath` (the `tiddlers/` directory). Watching additional paths would require modifying `watcher.js`.

!! Q: Why a red icon AND a yellow alert?

The yellow alert is a standard TW notification (tagged `$:/tags/Alert`) that tells you what changed. Once you dismiss the alert, there's no reminder that a reload is needed. The red refresh icon (via `body.fsw-reload-needed` CSS class) persists until you actually reload, so you don't forget.

!! Q: Does the write-lock ever cause problems?

The lock window is 1500ms. If you save a tiddler in the browser and then immediately save the same file externally within 1.5 seconds, the external change will be ignored. In practice this timing collision is extremely unlikely.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/overview","created":"20260305110636214","modified":"20260305110636214","rdt.hub-category":"infrastructure","rdt.hub-category-name":"Infrastructure","rdt.hub-order":"1","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

Watches the wiki's tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes (create, modify, delete) and syncs them into the running wiki in real-time using chokidar.

!! Features

* ''Live sync'' — file changes detected via chokidar, fed into `$tw.wiki` store
* ''No duplicates'' — updates `$tw.syncer.tiddlerInfo` to prevent syncer from re-saving
* ''Reload alerts'' — yellow alert + red refresh icon for system/module tiddlers
* ''Loop prevention'' — hooks `saveTiddlerToFile`/`deleteTiddlerFile` with write-lock
* ''Cross-platform'' — works on Windows, macOS, Linux
* ''Configurable poll interval'' via `config.tid`

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki running in Node.js server mode
* `chokidar` npm package installed
* Refresh button enabled in Control Panel > Settings > Toolbars > Page Toolbar

!! Quick start

# Install chokidar: `npm install chokidar`
# Start the server: `npx tiddlywiki mywiki --listen`
# Edit any tiddler file in `mywiki/tiddlers/` — changes appear within the configured poll interval

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">Regular tiddlers sync automatically. System tiddlers (`$:/` prefix) and module tiddlers trigger a reload alert because they don't sync to the browser by default.</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/reference","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","rdt.order":"6","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Config tiddlers

|!Tiddler |!Default |!Description |
|`$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` |`4000` |Browser polling interval in ms |
|`$:/config/SyncSystemTiddlersFromServer` |`"no"` |Whether system tiddlers sync (TW core, not plugin) |

!! State / temp tiddlers

|!Tiddler |!Set by |!Purpose |
|`alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload` |Server (watcher.js) |Yellow alert banner, tagged `$:/tags/Alert` |
|`$:/temp/filesystem-watcher-reload-needed` |Browser (reload-indicator.js) |Persists reload-needed state after alert dismissed |

!! CSS classes

|!Class |!Element |!Description |
|`fsw-reload-needed` |`document.body` |Added when reload is needed, removed on F5 |

!! Internal constants (watcher.js)

|!Constant |!Value |!Description |
|`WRITE_LOCK_MS` |`1500` |Ignore filesystem events within this window after TW writes |
|`DEBOUNCE_MS` |`200` |Debounce rapid filesystem events |
|`ALERT_TITLE` |`"alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload"` |Title of the reload alert tiddler |

!! chokidar config

|!Option |!Value |
|`ignored` |`/(^|[\/\\])\./` (dotfiles) |
|`persistent` |`true` |
|`ignoreInitial` |`true` |
|`awaitWriteFinish.stabilityThreshold` |`200` |
|`awaitWriteFinish.pollInterval` |`50` |

!! Hook points

|!Hooked function |!Purpose |
|`$tw.utils.saveTiddlerToFile` |Write-lock: tracks TW's own file writes |
|`$tw.utils.deleteTiddlerFile` |Write-lock: tracks TW's own file deletes |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/tw-internals":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/tw-internals","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","rdt.order":"7","rdt.plugin-key":"filesystem-watcher","rdt.plugin-name":"filesystem watcher","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Live sync of external filesystem changes into the running wiki","rdt.section":"TW Internals","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Boot and file loading

* `$tw.loadTiddlersFromFile(filepath)` — parses `.tid`/`.meta` files into tiddler objects
* `$tw.boot.files[title]` — maps tiddler titles to filesystem paths
* `$tw.boot.wikiTiddlersPath` — base path for the tiddlers directory

!! Wiki store

* `$tw.wiki.addTiddler(tiddler)` — adds/updates a tiddler in the store
* `$tw.wiki.deleteTiddler(title)` — removes a tiddler from the store
* `$tw.wiki.getChangeCount(title)` — returns the change counter for a tiddler

!! Server-side syncer

* `$tw.syncer.tiddlerInfo[title]` — tracks change counts per tiddler
* When `tiddlerInfo.changeCount` matches `wiki.getChangeCount()`, the syncer skips re-saving

!! Browser-side syncer

* Polls `GET /recipes/default/tiddlers.json` at the configured interval
* `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` controls the polling frequency
* `$:/config/SyncSystemTiddlersFromServer` defaults to `"no"` — system tiddlers don't sync

!! Filesystem adaptor

* `saveTiddlerToFile` / `deleteTiddlerFile` — internal methods hooked for loop prevention
* The watcher intercepts these to track which writes originated from TW itself

!! Alert mechanism

* Tiddlers tagged `$:/tags/Alert` render as yellow notification banners
* Pattern borrowed from TW's dirty indicator: `body.tc-dirty` → adapted as `body.fsw-reload-needed`

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">`$:/config/SyncSystemTiddlersFromServer` defaults to `"no"` — this is why system tiddlers need a full page reload to appear in the browser.</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/documentation":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/documentation","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<%if [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]has[plugin-type]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/overview">
<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/doc/overview"/>
</$tiddler>
<%else%>
To display this documentation, the plugin [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]] needs to be installed.

See the [[readme|$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/readme]] tab for a basic overview.
<%endif%>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/icon":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/icon","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","type":"image/svg+xml","text":"<svg width="22pt" height="22pt" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="maroon" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
<path d="M1 12s4-8 11-8 11 8 11 8-4 8-11 8S1 12 1 12z"/>
<circle cx="12" cy="12" r="3"/>
</svg>"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/readme","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! filesystem watcher

Watches the tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes (created, modified, deleted by tools like Claude Code) and syncs them into the running wiki in real time.

!! Key features

* ''Live file sync'' — detects file changes via chokidar and updates the wiki store automatically
* ''Reload alerts'' — yellow alert + red refresh icon for changes requiring a browser reload (module tiddlers, system tiddlers)
* ''Loop prevention'' — hooks TW's save/delete functions to ignore its own filesystem writes
* ''CLI mode skip'' — exits immediately when `TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE=1` so CLI imports don't hang
* ''Ignored tiddlers'' — skips noisy tiddlers like `$:/StoryList` that change frequently and have no value for external sync
* ''Configurable poll interval'' — set sync frequency in settings

!! Prerequisites

* chokidar (npm dependency, installed with the wiki)

!! Quick start

Install plugin. Configure the poll interval in ControlPanel > Settings > filesystem watcher. External file changes in the tiddlers directory sync automatically into the running wiki.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/reload-indicator.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/reload-indicator.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/reload-indicator.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: startup

Browser-side module that watches for the reload alert tiddler
and toggles a CSS class on document.body to turn the refresh icon red.
The class is gone after F5 since $:/temp tiddlers don't survive reload.

\*/

"use strict";

exports.name = "filesystem-watcher-reload-indicator";
exports.after = ["startup"];
exports.platforms = ["browser"];
exports.synchronous = true;

exports.startup = function() {
	$tw.wiki.addEventListener("change", function(changes) {
		if(changes["alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload"]) {
			if($tw.wiki.tiddlerExists("alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload")) {
				$tw.wiki.addTiddler({
					title: "$:/temp/filesystem-watcher-reload-needed",
					text: "yes"
				});
				document.body.classList.add("fsw-reload-needed");
			}
		}
	});
};
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"startup","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/settings":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/settings","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<div class="rr-settings-info-card">
No configurable settings. Future settings will appear here.
</div>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/styles":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/styles","created":"20260305110636215","modified":"20260305110636215","tags":"$:/tags/Stylesheet","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"body.fsw-reload-needed .tc-btn-\%24\%3A\%2Fcore\%2Fui\%2FButtons\%2Frefresh svg {
    fill: <<colour alert-highlight>>;
}
"},"test-watcher-logic.js":{"title":"test-watcher-logic.js","text":"/*\
title: test-watcher-logic.js

Jasmine tests for filesystem-watcher plugin.

The watcher.js and reload-indicator.js modules define their testable logic
inside the startup() closure, so we cannot call those functions directly.
Instead we:
  1. Verify module exports/metadata are correct.
  2. Extract and test the pure logic patterns (requiresReload,
     isSystemTiddler, isWatcherFile, alert text formatting) by
     re-implementing them exactly as written in watcher.js.

This ensures the logic stays correct if someone refactors or copies it,
and documents the expected behaviour.

\*/

"use strict";

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Pure logic extracted from watcher.js (closured — not directly importable)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function requiresReload(tiddlerFields) {
	return tiddlerFields.type === "application/javascript" &&
		tiddlerFields["module-type"];
}

function isSystemTiddler(title) {
	return title && title.indexOf("$:/") === 0;
}

function isWatcherFile(filepath) {
	// Uses path.basename equivalent — just grab last segment
	var basename = filepath.replace(/^.*[\/\\]/, "");
	return basename.indexOf("alerts_filesystem-watcher") === 0;
}

function formatAlertText(entries, count) {
	return "''Full reload required!'' " + count + " tiddler" + (count > 1 ? "s" : "") + " changed:\n\n" + entries;
}

function formatEntry(title, reason) {
	return "* ''" + title + "'' //" + reason + "//";
}

function countEntries(text) {
	return (text.match(/\* ''/g) || []).length;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tests
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

describe("filesystem-watcher module metadata", function() {

	describe("watcher.js exports", function() {
		var watcher;

		beforeEach(function() {
			// Read the raw source to inspect the export assignments.
			// We cannot require() it because it depends on $tw, chokidar, etc.
			var fs = require("fs");
			var path = require("path");
			var src = fs.readFileSync(
				path.resolve(__dirname, "..", "watcher.js"), "utf8"
			);
			watcher = { src: src };
		});

		it("should export name as 'filesystem-watcher'", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain('exports.name = "filesystem-watcher"');
		});

		it("should run after load-modules", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain('exports.after = ["load-modules"]');
		});

		it("should target node platform only", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain('exports.platforms = ["node"]');
		});

		it("should be asynchronous", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain("exports.synchronous = false");
		});

		it("should export a startup function", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain("exports.startup = function");
		});

		it("should check TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE for early exit", function() {
			expect(watcher.src).toContain('TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE');
		});
	});

	describe("reload-indicator.js exports", function() {
		var src;

		beforeEach(function() {
			var fs = require("fs");
			var path = require("path");
			src = fs.readFileSync(
				path.resolve(__dirname, "..", "reload-indicator.js"), "utf8"
			);
		});

		it("should export name as 'filesystem-watcher-reload-indicator'", function() {
			expect(src).toContain('exports.name = "filesystem-watcher-reload-indicator"');
		});

		it("should run after startup", function() {
			expect(src).toContain('exports.after = ["startup"]');
		});

		it("should target browser platform only", function() {
			expect(src).toContain('exports.platforms = ["browser"]');
		});

		it("should be synchronous", function() {
			expect(src).toContain("exports.synchronous = true");
		});

		it("should listen for the reload alert tiddler", function() {
			expect(src).toContain("alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload");
		});

		it("should set the fsw-reload-needed CSS class", function() {
			expect(src).toContain("fsw-reload-needed");
		});

		it("should write to a $:/temp/ tiddler", function() {
			expect(src).toContain("$:/temp/filesystem-watcher-reload-needed");
		});
	});
});

describe("requiresReload", function() {

	it("should return truthy for JS modules", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "application/javascript",
			"module-type": "startup"
		})).toBeTruthy();
	});

	it("should return truthy for any module-type value", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "application/javascript",
			"module-type": "widget"
		})).toBeTruthy();
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "application/javascript",
			"module-type": "route"
		})).toBeTruthy();
	});

	it("should return falsy for JS without module-type", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "application/javascript"
		})).toBeFalsy();
	});

	it("should return falsy for non-JS with module-type", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "text/vnd.tiddlywiki",
			"module-type": "startup"
		})).toBeFalsy();
	});

	it("should return falsy for plain tiddlers", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "text/vnd.tiddlywiki"
		})).toBeFalsy();
	});

	it("should return falsy for empty fields", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({})).toBeFalsy();
	});

	it("should return falsy when module-type is empty string", function() {
		expect(requiresReload({
			type: "application/javascript",
			"module-type": ""
		})).toBeFalsy();
	});
});

describe("isSystemTiddler", function() {

	it("should return true for $:/ prefixed titles", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler("$:/core/ui/ViewTemplate")).toBe(true);
	});

	it("should return true for $:/ alone", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler("$:/")).toBe(true);
	});

	it("should return false for normal titles", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler("MyTiddler")).toBe(false);
	});

	it("should return false for titles containing but not starting with $:/", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler("See $:/core for details")).toBe(false);
	});

	it("should return false for empty string", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler("")).toBeFalsy();
	});

	it("should return false for null/undefined", function() {
		expect(isSystemTiddler(null)).toBeFalsy();
		expect(isSystemTiddler(undefined)).toBeFalsy();
	});
});

describe("isWatcherFile", function() {

	it("should match the watcher alert file", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("/wiki/tiddlers/alerts_filesystem-watcher-reload.tid")).toBe(true);
	});

	it("should match with backslashes (Windows paths)", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("C:\\wiki\\tiddlers\\alerts_filesystem-watcher-reload.tid")).toBe(true);
	});

	it("should match any file starting with alerts_filesystem-watcher", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("/tiddlers/alerts_filesystem-watcher-foo.tid")).toBe(true);
	});

	it("should not match unrelated alert files", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("/tiddlers/alerts_something-else.tid")).toBe(false);
	});

	it("should not match a file with the pattern in a parent directory", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("/alerts_filesystem-watcher/other.tid")).toBe(false);
	});

	it("should not match normal tiddler files", function() {
		expect(isWatcherFile("/tiddlers/MyTiddler.tid")).toBe(false);
	});
});

describe("alert text formatting", function() {

	it("should use singular 'tiddler' for count 1", function() {
		var text = formatAlertText("* ''Foo'' //module//", 1);
		expect(text).toContain("1 tiddler changed");
		expect(text).not.toContain("tiddlers");
	});

	it("should use plural 'tiddlers' for count > 1", function() {
		var text = formatAlertText("* ''A'' //module//\n* ''B'' //system//", 2);
		expect(text).toContain("2 tiddlers changed");
	});

	it("should include the header with bold markup", function() {
		var text = formatAlertText("* ''X'' //m//", 1);
		expect(text).toContain("''Full reload required!''");
	});

	it("should separate header and entries with double newline", function() {
		var text = formatAlertText("* ''X'' //m//", 1);
		expect(text).toContain("changed:\n\n*");
	});
});

describe("formatEntry", function() {

	it("should wrap title in bold and reason in italic", function() {
		expect(formatEntry("$:/my/module", "module"))
			.toBe("* ''$:/my/module'' //module//");
	});

	it("should handle system reason", function() {
		expect(formatEntry("$:/config/Something", "system"))
			.toBe("* ''$:/config/Something'' //system//");
	});
});

describe("countEntries", function() {

	it("should count zero for empty text", function() {
		expect(countEntries("")).toBe(0);
	});

	it("should count one entry", function() {
		expect(countEntries("* ''Foo'' //module//")).toBe(1);
	});

	it("should count multiple entries", function() {
		var text = "* ''A'' //module//\n* ''B'' //system//\n* ''C'' //module//";
		expect(countEntries(text)).toBe(3);
	});
});

    "description": "Test edition for filesystem-watcher",
    "plugins": [
        "tiddlywiki/jasmine",
        "rimir/filesystem-watcher"
    ],

","type":".info"},"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/watcher.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/watcher.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher/watcher.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: startup

Watches the wiki tiddlers directory for external changes using chokidar
and syncs them into the in-memory wiki store.

\*/

"use strict";

exports.name = "filesystem-watcher";
exports.after = ["load-modules"];
exports.platforms = ["node"];
exports.synchronous = false;

exports.startup = function(callback) {
	// Option 3: self-aware CLI detection — skip watcher entirely in CLI mode
	if(process.env.TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE === "1") {
		callback();
		return;
	}

	var chokidar = require("chokidar"),
		path = require("path"),
		fs = require("fs");

	var logger = new $tw.utils.Logger("filesystem-watcher", {colour: "green"});
	var tiddlersPath = $tw.boot.wikiTiddlersPath;

	if(!tiddlersPath) {
		logger.log("No tiddlers path found, watcher not started");
		callback();
		return;
	}

	// Clear any stale alert from previous session
	$tw.wiki.deleteTiddler("alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload");

	// Set to track files recently written by TiddlyWiki itself (to avoid re-import loops)
	var recentlyWritten = {};
	var WRITE_LOCK_MS = 1500;

	// Debounce timers per file
	var debounceTimers = {};
	var DEBOUNCE_MS = 200;

	// Hook into TiddlyWiki's file save to track writes we should ignore
	var origSaveTiddlerToFile = $tw.utils.saveTiddlerToFile;
	$tw.utils.saveTiddlerToFile = function(tiddler, fileInfo, cb) {
		var filepath = fileInfo.filepath;
		if(filepath) {
			var normalized = path.resolve(filepath);
			recentlyWritten[normalized] = Date.now();
			// Also mark meta file if applicable
			if(fileInfo.hasMetaFile) {
				recentlyWritten[normalized + ".meta"] = Date.now();
			}
		}
		return origSaveTiddlerToFile.apply(this, arguments);
	};

	// Also hook into delete to ignore unlink events from TW's own deletions
	var origDeleteTiddlerFile = $tw.utils.deleteTiddlerFile;
	$tw.utils.deleteTiddlerFile = function(fileInfo, cb) {
		var filepath = fileInfo.filepath;
		if(filepath) {
			var normalized = path.resolve(filepath);
			recentlyWritten[normalized] = Date.now();
			if(fileInfo.hasMetaFile) {
				recentlyWritten[normalized + ".meta"] = Date.now();
			}
		}
		return origDeleteTiddlerFile.apply(this, arguments);
	};

	function isRecentlyWritten(filepath) {
		var normalized = path.resolve(filepath);
		var timestamp = recentlyWritten[normalized];
		if(timestamp && (Date.now() - timestamp) < WRITE_LOCK_MS) {
			return true;
		}
		delete recentlyWritten[normalized];
		return false;
	}

	// Find tiddler title by filepath in $tw.boot.files
	function findTitleByPath(filepath) {
		var normalized = path.resolve(filepath);
		for(var title in $tw.boot.files) {
			if($tw.boot.files[title].filepath && path.resolve($tw.boot.files[title].filepath) === normalized) {
				return title;
			}
		}
		return null;
	}

	// Alert tiddler title for reload notifications
	var ALERT_TITLE = "alerts/filesystem-watcher-reload";

	// Update the syncer's tiddlerInfo so it doesn't re-save tiddlers we just loaded from disk
	function updateSyncerInfo(title) {
		if($tw.syncer) {
			var tiddler = $tw.wiki.getTiddler(title);
			$tw.syncer.tiddlerInfo[title] = {
				changeCount: $tw.wiki.getChangeCount(title),
				adaptorInfo: $tw.syncer.syncadaptor ? $tw.syncer.syncadaptor.getTiddlerInfo(tiddler) : {},
				revision: $tw.wiki.getChangeCount(title)
			};
		}
	}

	function requiresReload(tiddlerFields) {
		return tiddlerFields.type === "application/javascript" &&
			tiddlerFields["module-type"];
	}

	function isSystemTiddler(title) {
		return title && title.indexOf("$:/") === 0;
	}

	function setReloadAlert(changedTitle, reason) {
		var existing = $tw.wiki.getTiddler(ALERT_TITLE);
		var entries;
		if(existing) {
			entries = existing.fields.text.replace(/^.*?:\n\n/,"");
			if(entries.indexOf("* ''" + changedTitle + "''") === -1) {
				entries = entries + "\n* ''" + changedTitle + "'' //" + reason + "//";
			}
		} else {
			entries = "* ''" + changedTitle + "'' //" + reason + "//";
		}
		var count = (entries.match(/\* ''/g) || []).length;
		$tw.wiki.addTiddler(new $tw.Tiddler({
			title: ALERT_TITLE,
			tags: "$:/tags/Alert",
			component: "filesystem-watcher",
			modified: $tw.utils.stringifyDate(new Date()),
			text: "''Full reload required!'' " + count + " tiddler" + (count > 1 ? "s" : "") + " changed:\n\n" + entries
		}));
		updateSyncerInfo(ALERT_TITLE);
		logger.log("Reload required for:", changedTitle, "(" + reason + ")");
	}

	// Tiddler titles to ignore — these change too frequently and have no value for external sync
	var IGNORED_TITLES = ["$:/StoryList"];

	function isWatcherFile(filepath) {
		return path.basename(filepath).indexOf("alerts_filesystem-watcher") === 0;
	}

	function isIgnoredTitle(filepath) {
		var title = findTitleByPath(filepath);
		return title && IGNORED_TITLES.indexOf(title) !== -1;
	}

	function handleFileChange(filepath) {
		if(isWatcherFile(filepath) || isRecentlyWritten(filepath) || isIgnoredTitle(filepath)) {
			return;
		}
		try {
			if(!fs.existsSync(filepath)) {
				return;
			}
			var tiddlers = $tw.loadTiddlersFromFile(filepath);
			if(tiddlers && tiddlers.tiddlers) {
				$tw.utils.each(tiddlers.tiddlers, function(tiddlerFields) {
					var title = tiddlerFields.title;
					if(title) {
						// Update boot.files mapping
						$tw.boot.files[title] = {
							filepath: filepath,
							type: tiddlers.type || "application/x-tiddler",
							hasMetaFile: tiddlers.hasMetaFile || false
						};
						$tw.wiki.addTiddler(new $tw.Tiddler(tiddlerFields));
						updateSyncerInfo(title);
						logger.log("Reloaded:", title);
						// Check if this tiddler requires a full browser reload
						if(requiresReload(tiddlerFields)) {
							setReloadAlert(title, "module");
						} else if(isSystemTiddler(title)) {
							setReloadAlert(title, "system");
						}
					}
				});
			}
		} catch(e) {
			logger.log("Error loading file " + filepath + ": " + e.message);
		}
	}

	function handleFileDelete(filepath) {
		if(isWatcherFile(filepath) || isRecentlyWritten(filepath) || isIgnoredTitle(filepath)) {
			return;
		}
		var title = findTitleByPath(filepath);
		if(title) {
			delete $tw.boot.files[title];
			$tw.wiki.deleteTiddler(title);
			if($tw.syncer) {
				delete $tw.syncer.tiddlerInfo[title];
			}
			logger.log("Deleted:", title);
		}
	}

	function debouncedChange(filepath) {
		var key = path.resolve(filepath);
		if(debounceTimers[key]) {
			clearTimeout(debounceTimers[key]);
		}
		debounceTimers[key] = setTimeout(function() {
			delete debounceTimers[key];
			handleFileChange(filepath);
		}, DEBOUNCE_MS);
	}

	function debouncedDelete(filepath) {
		var key = path.resolve(filepath);
		if(debounceTimers[key]) {
			clearTimeout(debounceTimers[key]);
		}
		debounceTimers[key] = setTimeout(function() {
			delete debounceTimers[key];
			handleFileDelete(filepath);
		}, DEBOUNCE_MS);
	}

	// Start watching
	var watcher = chokidar.watch(tiddlersPath, {
		ignored: [/(^|[\/\\])\./, /[\/\\]ext-outbox[\/\\]/],  // ignore dotfiles + ext-outbox (handled by Python connector)
		persistent: true,
		ignoreInitial: true,       // don't fire for existing files on startup
		awaitWriteFinish: {
			stabilityThreshold: 200,
			pollInterval: 50
		}
	});

	watcher
		.on("add", function(filepath) {
			debouncedChange(filepath);
		})
		.on("change", function(filepath) {
			debouncedChange(filepath);
		})
		.on("unlink", function(filepath) {
			debouncedDelete(filepath);
		})
		.on("ready", function() {
			logger.log("Watching for external changes in:", tiddlersPath);
			callback();
		})
		.on("error", function(error) {
			logger.log("Watcher error:", error.message);
		});
};
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"startup","created":"20260305110636216","modified":"20260305110636216"}}}
